atlas.put_in: overdamped Langevin in an asymmetric periodic potential + symmetric drive +/-A + noise
emerged: symmetric potential: rocking current ~0 ([0.0004, 0.0001, 0.0005, -0.0008]); asymmetric ratchet: [0.0186, 0.1892, 0.6032, 0.9574] (grows with A)
rocking current vs asymmetry at A=4.5: [0.0008, 0.0779, 0.2261, 0.4219, 0.6058] (any asymmetry rectifies)
surprises: a SYMMETRIC vibration yields a definite-sign net current once the potential is asymmetric
atlas.persistence: the rectification sign is fixed by the potential asymmetry; magnitude grows with drive
atlas.not_scripted_check: no direction is put in; only V's left/right asymmetry; the net current is measured
atlas.claim_tier: measured (rectification, growth, any-asymmetry) ; interpretive (the motor's arrow = broken spatial symmetry) ; analogy (bolt/ratchet)
floors: 1D overdamped point particle; absolute currents depend on (D, T, dt)
the SIGN and growth of the rectification are the gated facts, not the magnitude
'bolt tightening' is analogy, not a claim about a real fastener
